item.title: Burberry tried to be Louis Vuitton; it should aim lower
item.description: The historic brand’s rarefied look didn’t sell; and it is returning to its classic trench coats, scarves and check pattern

item.title: Israeli leaders threaten New York Times with lawsuit
item.description: Benjamin Netanyahu says he will take legal action over article detailing sexual abuse of Palestinians by Israeli prison guards

item.title: Jaguar Land Rover swings to an annual loss after cyber attack and US tariffs
item.description: Tata Motors-owned luxury car maker’s chief executive vows to make it ‘more resilient’

item.title: UK company linked to Kazakh oligarch found liable in employment tribunal
item.description: Financial services group Jusan withheld termination fee from former chief operating officer, judge rules

item.title: Reader callout: Do you regret buying your home?
item.description: Some buyers find themselves living in properties bought during the pandemic rush but have come to regret their decision.

item.title: Hargreaves Lansdown to cut jobs in bid to fight off digital rivals
item.description: UK’s biggest DIY investment platform plans to spend £100mn to improve products and digital services

item.title: US audit regulator weighs deep staff cuts to unit overseeing accounting firms
item.description: PCAOB is taking less confrontational approach as it downsizes Enron-era mandate to scrutinise industry

item.title: China’s Weichai ousts management at Italian yachtmaker
item.description: Vote on competing board slates caps months-long fight between two largest investors at Ferretti

item.title: Tate & Lyle surges on £2.7bn Ingredion takeover bid
item.description: Shares in London-listed ingredients company jump after announcement of cash bid from US peer

item.title: Renault’s European manufacturing better than China’s, chief says
item.description: François Provost claims superior ‘efficiency’ as carmaker speeds up research and development

item.title: Klarna breaks even for first time since blockbuster New York IPO
item.description: Shares in European fintech have plunged 70% since September

item.title: China warns UK over nationalisation of British Steel
item.description: Beijing says it will take ‘strong measures’ to protect rights of owner Jingye

item.title: AI desperately needs more adult supervision
item.description: The critical challenge is to build institutions that protect us from tech companies and the state

item.title: Is an AI spending plateau on the horizon?
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: carbon capture shows promise in decarbonising data centres

item.title: NHS hospitals hit key target on waiting times in boost for Streeting
item.description: Cut in waiting lists in England revealed ahead of health secretary’s expected bid for Labour leadership

item.title: ‘I’ve seen some things’: Eric Ries on why good companies go bad
item.description: Author of the start-up bible has turned his attention to corrupting influences at the heart of contemporary capitalism

item.title: Carmakers fear production hit after EU sanctions on Chinese chipmaker
item.description: Companies ‘scrambling for information’ after ban on trading with key supplier

item.title: AI alone cannot shorten the work week
item.description: The technology could raise prices and consumption before it gives us more free time

item.title: King’s Cross is the Silicon Roundabout of AI
item.description: A formerly rundown area has become London’s new global technology hub

item.title: Cuba runs out of diesel and fuel oil
item.description: Protests erupt as energy minister admits island has ‘absolutely no’ supplies or reserves

item.title: Abu Dhabi’s L’imad joins BlackRock and Temasek on $30bn infrastructure push
item.description: Alliance underlines growing influence of emirate’s newest sovereign wealth fund

item.title: Revolut wins UK approval to offer more complex financial products
item.description: Europe’s largest fintech is looking to expand its appeal among wealthy customers

item.title: War disruption grows and data centres go to space
item.description: The inside story on the Asia tech trends that matter, from Nikkei Asia and the Financial Times

item.title: British navy says vessel seized off UAE coast and heading for Iran
item.description: Ship anchored north-east of Fujairah outside Strait of Hormuz taken by ‘unauthorised personnel’

item.title: UK landlord Landsec says occupancy levels at 20-year high
item.description: FTSE 100 group posts stronger than expected growth in rental income

item.title: Spire Healthcare shares soar on £1bn takeover offer
item.description: Activist investor Toscafund offers £2.50 a share for UK’s largest private hospital operator

item.title: Burberry returns to profit as turnaround gains ground
item.description: Luxury group warns on effect of Iran war on some regional sales

item.title: 3i shares tumble after sales growth slows at discount retailer Action
item.description: Private equity group has relied on Dutch retailer to power its returns in recent years

item.title: Honda slumps to first annual loss on $9bn EV charge
item.description: Japan’s second-biggest carmaker reports worse than expected result but forecasts return to profit this year

item.title: Mortgage refixing, longer lags and eggs
item.description: Plus, US oil producers aren’t drilling
